subject: grammar
title: Prepositions: Formal Letter
instruction: Fill in the gaps in the letter with the correct prepositions.
passage:
Dear Mr Rees,
Thank you for your letter of 21 June. I was very disturbed 1 by (by) your account of what happened in our hotel last week, and I am writing to apologise 2 [2] this. I can understand that you were shocked 3 [3] the way the employee spoke to you. There can be no excuse 4 [4] such behaviour and I fully understand why you felt the need to complain 5 [5] the situation. I can provide no explanation 6 [6] the way the receptionist behaved.

Our hotel group has a reputation 7 [7] being good 8 [8] looking after our customers, and I am very sorry that your experience 9 [9] our service did not confirm this. I intend to deal 10 [10] the staff member appropriately and I will ensure that she does not repeat this behaviour 11 [11] sending her on a retraining course next week. I would like to assure you that the behaviour you encountered is not typical 12 [12] our staff.

As the manager, I am responsible 13 [13] all of our employees so please accept my apologies again and this voucher worth £50 to spend in any 14 [14] our hotels.

Yours sincerely,
Clive Martin
Hotel Manager
